Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and marked proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria in urine, suggesting possible diabetic nephropathy or acute kidney injury. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal involvement. Liver enzymes and electrolytes are within normal limits. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for diabetes and renal pathology.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
